name: Re-Lock The Single-K 128x128 Hot-Band Default

summary.name: Re-Lock The Single-K 128x128 Hot-Band Default
summary.hypothesis: Round 15/50 has no extra user-authored idea family in `state/human_review.md` beyond the approval gate and the requirement that node_c implement exactly one direction, so the best next move is a single-family rollback of the just-measured negative. The latest run `20260420_194440_bf16_gemm_v1_cb070a7` regressed from `25.504256 ms` to `31.797344 ms` after promoting `bf16_gemm_v1_tensor_core_fixed_hot_band_128x128x32_kernel`, and the dominant hot-band kernel now carries `212` regs/thread, `43,008` B shared/block, register/shared occupancy caps of `2/2`, `10.06%` barrier stall, and `10.27%`/`5.03%` L1 bank read/write pressure while tensor activity sits at only `39.88%`. That points to two-K stage depth and its extra shared/export lifetime as the primary regression source, so the highest-upside low-risk fix is to make the default fixed-shape path use the prior single-K `128x128` hot-band kernel again.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy plus shared-memory/barrier pressure inside the two-K `128x128x32` hot-band kernel, not the residual `64x384` rows or the `64x96` tail.
summary.risk: Low to moderate. The single-K `128x128` branch is already in-tree and was the immediate predecessor to this regression, but rolling back stage depth may only recover the `25.504256 ms` surface rather than the older `24.422464 ms` accepted best if another drift remains.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the current `31.797344 ms`, the prior `25.504256 ms`, and the accepted `24.422464 ms`
summary.stop_condition: Stop if reverting to the single-K `128x128` default does not recover close to the previous `25.504256 ms` result or if the hot-band kernel still stays near `212` regs/thread and `43,008` B shared/block after the swap.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
